title: Chinese Macroeconomic Trends and their Impact on the Dry Bulk Freight Market
section_path: Chinese Macroeconomic Trends and their Impact on the Dry Bulk Freight Market
section_path_text: Chinese Macroeconomic Trends and their Impact on the Dry Bulk Freight Market
summary:
Main
The dry bulk shipping market has performed startlingly well this year thus far, with a combination of unique factors to have enabled this strong momentum: port congestion, quarantine measures, geopolitics, tight tonnage supply, firm demand, all create a unique ecosystem in which this dry bulk rally is being cultivated. The question is: where do we go from here? The freight market is making history galloping at decade highs, with especially the Capesize segment producing some dazzling fixtures these past few weeks, while the Chinese celebrations for the National Day and the festivities for the Golden Week did not put a break on the upward momentum.

children.title: Main
children.summary: The dry bulk shipping market has performed startlingly well this year thus far, with a combination of unique factors to have enabled this strong momentum: port congestion, quarantine measures, geopolitics, tight tonnage supply, firm demand, all create a unique ecosystem in which this dry bulk rally is being cultivated. The question is: where do we go from here?

children.title: Concerns on China growth
children.summary: Amid a dry bulk freight euphoria, China is undergoing a power supply shock, the Evergrande crisis is looming and credit has tightened allowing for many speculations with regard to the course of the Chinese economy. Goldman Sachs has lowered its growth forecast for the Chinese economy to 7.8% (from 8.2% previously) on the basis of significant energy shortages.

children.title: Iron ore bull market nearing an end?
section_path: Iron ore bull market nearing an end?
children.section_path_text: Iron ore bull market nearing an end?
children.summary: China's unstable property and construction landscape in tandem with the country's consistent efforts to curb steel production, tame demand for iron ore and control its price, have made the red alloy subject to heavy volatility. Iron ore has been trading in a tight range of US$100-120 per metric tonne, levels not seen since July of 2020.

children.title: Beijing's Green push
children.summary: The Chinese authorities are intensively trying to meet their annual carbon reduction targets and are also trying to limit pollution levels ahead of the Winter Olympics which will take place in February next year. Further, the authorities have been pushing steel mills since early July to implement output and capacity curbs and have clearly laid out the goal: full year's 2021 crude steel production must remain at least flat (if not sub) to last year's record output of 1.065 billion tonnes.

children.title: The power supply crisis - a bull for coal
section_path: The power supply crisis - a bull for coal
children.section_path_text: The power supply crisis - a bull for coal
children.summary: China and India are both undergoing a severe power supply crisis with many provinces facing electricity shortages signalling augmented seaborne imports in the near-term. This phenomenon of serious electricity shortage has been triggered by a clash between the resurgence in industrial growth and the rebound in energy demand versus tight commodity supply, rising coal and gas prices and expensive freight costs.

children.title: Prospects
children.summary: The well documented "frenzy" in the dry bulk shipping sphere has admittedly created a generalised positive psychology for market participants and industry players, also reflected in the futures marks and some strong period fixtures reported across the board. It is this time though that we feel a balanced opinion which factors in all the important metrics, can bring actual value to the industry stakeholders.
